title: Caricina divulsa
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Caricina divulsa, it is possible that this plant is not well-known or is misspelled. However, I found information about the plant Carica papaya, which is a related species. Here's a paragraph about Carica papaya:

Carica papaya, commonly known as papaya or Caricina is a tropical plant species that belongs to the family Caricaceae. Carica papaya has been used for its numerous health benefits, including treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Carica papaya include papain, an enzyme with proteolytic activity, as well as vitamins A and C, potassium, and flavonoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Carica papaya include juice, tea, and capsule supplements, as well as topical creams and ointments used to treat skin conditions and wounds.

properties: Unfortunately, I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Carcina divulsa." However, I think you may be referring to "Carex divulsa," also known as Divided Sedge. If that is correct, Caricina divulsa, or more accurately, Carex divulsa, is considered to have various medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and stems are also rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may contribute to its therapeutic benefits.
